developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear the experience didn’t meet your expectations. Touring Karts is designed as an arcade-style game, so the physics are intentionally exaggerated to deliver fast, chaotic fun. With a bit of practice, it becomes much more intuitive.

Regarding the dominant hand setting: if you select "right," we assume you want to shoot with that hand and drive with the other. If that’s not what you intended, just select the opposite and it should work as expected.

Thanks for the feedback — it helps us improve!

reviews.review_description: What's the difference between this and pro version
Did 1 lap said motion sickness was off i had motion sickness doing 1 lap could never play this game.

developer_response.body: Hi! Many players are immune to motion sickness, but we understand your concern. In this game, you can select the recreational view, which is specifically designed to make motion sickness impossible. We hope you'll give it another try!

reviews.review_title: Pro now need to buy another version?
reviews.review_description:
What's with the Pro now?

I think I paid already, may I'm mixing it up with Galaxy kart.

developer_response.body:
Hi! 😊
There are two versions of Touring Karts:

The free version, fully playable but with limited content.

The PRO version, a paid one with all content unlocked from the start (more tracks, more karts, etc.).

If you already have one version, you don’t need to buy the other, unless you want instant access to all the extra features.
Hope you're enjoying the ride! 🏁

reviews.review_title: I can’t tell if this is a good game
reviews.review_description: I’ve had this game for several months but have been unable to play it because when it starts I’m going backwards. I’ve uninstalled it  several times, changed to joystick and said a few hail Mary’s. Nothing. I really wish I could get my money back. I ever had a friend try it and she was just as stuck as I was.

It's free so you may as well try it anyways but I'll try to speak purely to quality here.

Much like another reviewer I was met with an infinite black screen the first time I tried to load the app. Restarting it fixed the issue, however.

The game itself is very rough. I love kart racers: Mario Kart 8 Deluxe is one of my favorite games and Dash Dash World is one of my favorite VR games. Touring Karts however isn't nearly as good as that.

The handling is very weird. Drifting usually feels satisfying in kart racers but here it feels like it puts your kart on ice. Courses are designed around it but it's unsatisfying. Getting hit by weapons is frustrating because there's usually no indication of what hit you: you'll just stop or spin or slow down. Dash Dash World fixes this by adding little character portraits showing who hit who with what along with other visual indicators so it's clear what's happening.

The courses are largely uninteresting so far. They try to mix kart racing and formula 1 but it ends up satisfying neither: they're too bland for a kart racer, but not technical enough for a regular racer.

Graphics are also pretty rough even for a Quest 1 game. I hate to keep comparing it given one is paid and the other isn't but DDW has better graphics with similar inconsistent performance.

To say something nice, again it's free so you may as well try it and I'll keep trying to see if something *clicks* for me and the idea of combining weapons seems fun. However, if you're looking for a great VR kart racer, expect to look elsewhere.

reviews.review_title: How the heck is this game FREE?!?!
reviews.review_description: The devs have given us a real gift here. Such a fun cart racing game with all sorts of fun powerups and combos. powerups are grabbed out of the air with your hand and physically thrown at opponents. Wide variety of tracks to drive on, each with it's own unique local and flare. Even parts of the tracks elevate as you drive. Lots of vehicles to unlock and you can play all the "missions" and unlock vehicle upgrades for free. So certainly not pay to win. The only thing that's behind the "paywall" are visual/audible cosmetics. And some of the money to do your upgrades, which you can get by going pro... or playing multiplayer. A brilliant way to get free players to play online as much as possible, giving paid users more people to play with and show off all their bling. The steering options are motion controls or joystick. The motion controls are really more of a controller tilt using gyroscopics, which honestly, works really good in this case. Otherwise... you might lose tracking while you drive and are looking around. Takes a couple minutes to get used to, but it's extremely accurate.

Devs if you’re reading this please throw your computer out the window￼. Devs most of the five stars are from yourself￼￼ please put some effort into this game I believe you can but right now it’s kind of crap.￼

developer_response.body: With such constructive and respectful feedback it is a pleasure to continue working on the game for free.

reviews.review_description:
Holy Mother of God in Heaven above ....
WHAT GARBAGE.

I start to run it, then a notice comes up about mic access.
Kinda weird to still see that in this stage of Quest's life.
But whatever.
Shoulda taken that as an omen ....

After I ALLOW the mic access, all I get is the three loading dots ....
That wouldn't stop.
I tried quitting the app, but it wouldn't let me.
So I had to restart the whole Quest 2 ....

Then a notice pops up that there's an update for it!
"Great!", thought I.
"I guess they got so many complaints, they rushed out a patch."
Of course, the question is: why'd they release it in this state in the first place ...?
But, again, whatever ....

So I download the update and try again.
I get the same three loading dots.
Only THIS time, it fades to an eternal black screen.

ENOUGH.
Insta-Delete.
Stay away from this POS and don't give yourself a splitting headache!!
🤮🤮🤮

developer_response.body: The Mic access is for the chat voice multiplayer. You can allow it or not. About updates: We have not released any updates to the game and it works without problems. Could you try again? It seems like some one-off problem with your Oculus Quest.

developer_response.body: We let people play for free a game that has cost us a lot to do without time limits or levels. We put some advantages for people who want to help us by buying the PRO. I didn't understand your complaint until I saw your name and I remembered your threatening emails for not wanting to give you a key for the paid version for free. Too bad you ruin our work for no reason. I encourage you to turn the page because you are playing with what you feed us.

It's an okay game but I do the steering was better, I wish we could choose to grip the wheel and steer like other racing games for example v racing where you steer by grabbing the wheel and moving your controller while in this game one hand is automaticallygripped to the wheel, but that's kinda it, it is fun although for me it did kinda bug where the character in the tutorial kept on talking but had no dialog but yeah it's okay but could be better for more immersion

developer_response.body:
The control you explain forces you to hold down the grip (it's uncomfortable), and it doesn't work well in situations as crazy as those experienced in Touring Karts, where you unintentionally move your hand out of place and move the steering wheel wrong.
Our control is somewhat less "cool" at the beginning, but it is much more comfortable when you have been playing for a while, and above all, it does not do strange things even if the position of the hand is not correct.

reviews.review_title: Wanted to like but actually quite boring
reviews.review_description:
After playing a bunch of racing games over the years, this falls very short of exciting or even fun.
The controls are not really immersive or realistic & once you get the hang of steering, you can go round the circuit at your own leisure. Not much happens.
I imagined a VR Mario kart… sadly it’s not like that.
PS: Motion sickness warning. It’s a doozie if you can’t drive steady enough. I hit a dirt mound in the game & went from hot cinnamon donuts to rotten prawns in your guts in about 1 & 1/2 a second.

reviews.review_title: It’s good but needs some work

developer_response.body: The game includes all the methods to avoid motion sickness. Including the arcade view with which it is impossible to get dizzy. If you want to enjoy the game in the first person you have to get used to it with short game sessions. in any case, it is not a problem of the game
